subject: Sociology
title: Science and Disenchantment
description: A quick win: understand Science and Disenchantment.

hook.text: Lightning once meant God was angry. Now it means charged particles. Weber called this disenchantment.

buildup.text: Weber argued that rationalization strips the world of mystery. Science explains what religion once did.

discovery.text: Disenchantment doesn't kill religion outright. It pushes faith into personal life, away from the public.

twist.text: Yet enchantment keeps returning. Astrology apps boom. Crystals sell. People crave mystery beside science.

climax.text: Disenchantment created a meaning vacuum. New spiritual movements rush in to fill what science cannot.

punchline.text: Reason can't satisfy every human need.

quiz.question: What did Weber mean by 'disenchantment'?
options: Rationalization removing mystery from the world
